original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: Academy of Medical Royal Colleges Evidence-Based Interventions programme
original_registry_entry.version: EBI statutory guidance; last reviewed September 2024
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: England, United Kingdom; international commissioning and clinical evidence requiring UAE ENT adaptation
original_registry_entry.population: Adults and children with recurrent severe episodes of sore throat due to acute tonsillitis; exclusions and specialist conditions are explicit.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Primary and ENT assessment of recurrent tonsillitis and shared-decision documentation.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for episode history, documented clinically significant sore throats, functional impact, complications, shared decision, and distinguishing recurrent tonsillitis from sleep apnoea, suspected cancer, quinsy, and emergency presentations. It does not generate a tonsillectomy recommendation.

exact_sections.heading: Summary and recommendation — recurrent episodes, documentation, and functional impact
exact_sections.locator: official guidance Summary and Recommendation sections
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting recurrent sore-throat episodes attributed by a clinician to acute tonsillitis, episode history, normal-function impact, work or school impact, and complications without applying an automatic procedural threshold.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendation and exclusions — shared decision and separate pathways
exact_sections.locator: official guidance Recommendation and exclusion list
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording a clinician-completed benefit-risk-alternative discussion and recognizing sleep-disordered breathing, suspected cancer, recurrent quinsy, emergency presentations, immune deficiency, and other specialist contexts without recommending surgery.

title: Tonsillectomy for recurrent tonsillitis
publisher: Academy of Medical Royal Colleges Evidence-Based Interventions programme
jurisdiction: England, United Kingdom; international commissioning and clinical evidence requiring UAE ENT adaptation
population: Adults and children with recurrent severe episodes of sore throat due to acute tonsillitis; exclusions and specialist conditions are explicit.
intended_setting: Primary and ENT assessment of recurrent tonsillitis and shared-decision documentation.
